zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward or Ailward de Bury於大約1145年出生在英國藍卡爾波利時，他的父親Sir James de Bury16歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Crossman16歲。他和Alice de Montbegon至少有1個兒子。他去世在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward or Ailward de Bury于大约1145年出生在英国藍卡爾波利时，他的父亲Sir James de Bury16岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Crossman16岁。他和Alice de Montbegon至少有1个儿子。他去世在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Bury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Buri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Markus, Kurt.
Swiss German and South German: from an Alemannic pet form of the personal name Burkhard (see Burkhart ).
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Buro  .

variants.name: Burgh
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Ailward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Aylward  .

variants.name: Aylward
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name, Ailward, Old English Æthelweard, composed of elements meaning ‘noble’ + ‘guardian’. This English name has been established in Ireland since at least the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward or Ailward de Bury was born about 1145, in Bury, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir James de Bury, was 16 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Crossman, was 16. He had at least 1 son with Alice de Montbegon. He died in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward or Ailward de Bury est né environ 1145, à Bury, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir James de Bury, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Crossman, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Alice de Montbegon. Il est décédé à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward or Ailward de Bury um 1145 in Bury, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir James de Bury 16 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Crossman 16. Er hatte mit Alice de Montbegon mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward or Ailward de Bury è nato il circa 1145 nel Bury, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Sir James de Bury, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Crossman, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Alice de Montbegon. Morì nel England.

ja.Bury, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Edward or Ailward de Buryが約1145年にイギリスランカシュアイアブリーで生まれた時，父Sir James de Buryは16歳，母Lady Elizabeth Crossmanは16歳であった。彼はAlice de Montbegonとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼はイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward or Ailward de Bury(이)가 대략 1145년 영국 랭카실르 버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir James de Bury(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Crossman(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Alice de Montbegon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward or Ailward de Bury nasceu aproximadamente 1145, em Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir James de Bury, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Crossman, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Alice de Montbegon. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward or Ailward de Bury родился около 1145 г. в местности бури, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir James de Bury было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Crossman, 16. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Alice de Montbegon. Он умер в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward or Ailward de Bury nació aproximadamente 1145, en Bury, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir James de Bury, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Crossman, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Alice de Montbegon. Murió en Inglaterra.
